source_jobs.title: Remote Wedding Officiant
source_jobs.description:
Courtly is the leader in online marriage—making it possible for any couple around the world to get legally married online, 24/7. From obtaining a marriage license to performing the ceremony, Courtly is bringing the aisle online for couples in the 21st century—regardless of their nationality, sexuality, or physical location.

As a small, distributed team, we care deeply about building a culture around trust, diversity, and performance. You will have significant independence and are expected to have the autonomy to think on your feet and speak/act thoughtfully on behalf of the company.

Our network of licensed officiants remotely attend and perform marriage ceremonies for couples around the world. Experience officiating weddings is not required.

You will learn how to use our technology to officiate weddings in a remote-first manner and Courtly will provide a steady stream of ceremonies for you to officiate and earn money from. The ideal candidate is outgoing and compassionate and is motivated by making couples feel extra special on their big day.

Requirements

 - To officiate for Courtly, you must live in Utah County and be at least 21 years old

 - Strong proficiency in technology

 - You’ll need to submit a valid ID, photo, and proof of residency

 - Proficient in public speaking and hosting, excellent verbal communication skills

 - Experience officiating weddings not required

Responsibilities

 - Remotely attend couple's wedding ceremony through video conference

 - Follow pre-approved script and lead ceremony including vows, ring exchanges, and more

 - Correctly sign and file marriage license within 24 hours after virtual ceremony

Courtly is an equal opportunity employer and we value diversity. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We don't care about where you went to college, your degree, your age, or what fancy names you might have on your resume. Instead, we're looking for good people: good at work and with a good heart 💖

source_jobs.description:

You'll work closely with product managers, engineers, and stakeholders to deliver designs that balance user needs with business objectives.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Collaborate with product managers to understand user requirements and business goals

 - Work closely with engineers to ensure precise implementation of designs

 - Create wireframes, prototypes, and high-fidelity mockups

 - Participate in user research and incorporate feedback into design iterations

REQUIREMENTS

 - 3+ years of experience designing interfaces for B2B/enterprise software products

source_jobs.title: Freelance CG Artist - Product Rendering & Video
source_jobs.description:
We are a startup building AI agents and consumer devices. Users prompt agents to take actions across their tools, and use our custom devices to capture intent on the go. We are a small team based in Shenzhen and New York and our founder previously started a unicorn company in Silicon Valley.

We are looking for a few freelance CG artists to help create high-quality product images and videos for our smart ring. You will work from product references, CAD/STP files, CMF direction, and factory samples to produce polished renders, short videos, turntables, and motion assets for website, ads, social, investor materials, and product decision-making.

This is a freelance role. We care most about taste, speed, realism, motion sense, and the ability to turn loose direction into strong visual output without heavy hand-holding.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create photorealistic renders of a small consumer hardware product, primarily a smart ring.

 - Produce product images and videos for website, ads, social, pitch decks, and internal design reviews.

 - Create short product videos, turntables, motion tests, launch visuals, and social/ad clips.

 - Animate camera moves, product reveals, material transitions, LED states, charging moments, and simple interaction concepts.

 - Build realistic material studies across ceramic, metal, resin, glass, plastic, LED, and charging/contact details.

 - Create multiple CMF and finish explorations quickly from loose references.

 - Work with CAD/STP files, product photos, factory drawings, and reference images.

 - Set up clean lighting, camera angles, surfaces, and compositions that make a small wearable product feel premium.

 - Deliver organized source files and export-ready still/video assets in clean, reusable formats.

 - Iterate quickly based on feedback from product, marketing, and hardware teams.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Strong portfolio of product rendering work, ideally consumer electronics, wearables, jewelry, watches, rings, or small hardware.

 - Strong portfolio of product video, animation, motion graphics, or short 3D product ads.

 - Excellent sense of lighting, materials, composition, scale, and product detail.

 - Experience with Blender, Cinema 4D, KeyShot, Redshift, Octane, V-Ray, After Effects, Premiere, DaVinci Resolve, or similar rendering/video tools.

 - Comfortable working from imperfect inputs: CAD files, rough product references, factory samples, and written direction.

 - Able to move quickly and produce polished outputs without a large creative team around you.

 - Strong file organization and ability to deliver final images, final videos, and editable project files.

 - Good written communication and responsiveness.


HELPFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED

 - Experience rendering jewelry, ceramic, titanium, PVD finishes, transparent/translucent materials, or compact electronics.

 - Ability to combine 3D renders with AI video/image tools where useful.

 - Familiarity with DFM, CMF, industrial design, or factory communication.

 - Experience using AI image/video tools as part of a rendering or concept workflow.

 - Ability to lightly retouch or composite final outputs in Photoshop.


WHAT WE CARE ABOUT

 - Can you make a tiny object look expensive, real, desirable, and dynamic?

 - Can you create several visual directions fast, not just one perfect render slowly?

 - Can you create motion that explains the product without making it feel like a generic tech ad?

 - Can you understand product constraints without needing every detail specified?

 - Can your work help us make product and marketing decisions, not just look nice?


APPLICATION QUESTIONS

 - Portfolio: link to your best product rendering and product video work.

 - Tools: what software/render engines/video tools do you use?

 - Hardware experience: have you rendered jewelry, wearables, consumer electronics, or small physical products before?

 - Workflow: if we give you a STEP file, product references, and CMF notes, what is your usual p

source_jobs.title: General Manager - Portfolio Company
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AVIARA

Aviara is a private equity shop focused on AI-enabled rollups in emerging markets. We buy durable, founder-led businesses where local trust already exists, then improve them with better operating cadence, sales discipline, AI automation, and sharper financial management.

Our initial focus is Argentina and Latin America. We are looking at service businesses, software-enabled services, professional services, niche B2B operators, and legacy local companies where the owner has built something real but the business still runs on manual workflows, informal sales, spreadsheets, WhatsApp, and founder memory.

We are not hiring a strategist to write slides from the outside. We are hiring someone who can run one of the businesses we acquire.


ABOUT THE ROLE

The General Manager will be responsible for the day-to-day performance of an Aviara portfolio company.

You will inherit a real business with customers, employees, habits, constraints, and local reputation. Your job is to keep what works, fix what does not, and make the company more valuable every week. That means customer calls, sales follow-up, hiring, firing, pricing, collections, service delivery, process cleanup, reporting, and hands-on AI/process implementation.

This is a tactical operating role. You should be comfortable being close to the details: reviewing the pipeline, calling customers, checking cash, sitting with employees to understand workflows, installing simple systems, and making decisions quickly.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Run the weekly operating cadence: sales pipeline, customer health, delivery, staffing, cash, receivables, priorities, and blockers.

 - Own revenue, retention, gross margin, customer satisfaction, and basic P&L performance.

 - Spend time with customers to understand why they buy, why they stay, what frustrates them, and where expansion is possible.

 - Clean up the sales motion: lead tracking, follow-up, pricing, proposals, close rates, renewals, referrals, and account expansion.

 - Manage the team directly. Set clear expectations, coach people, hire where needed, and make hard people decisions when required.

 - Map the company's core workflows from lead to cash: how work comes in, how it gets assigned, how it gets delivered, how customers are billed, and where things break.

 - Identify the first practical AI/process improvements, such as call summaries, customer follow-up, quote generation, document processing, CRM hygiene, reporting, scheduling, collections, internal knowledge search, or repetitive admin work.

 - Implement simple tools and workflows that employees actually use.

 - Build dashboards and weekly reports that show what is happening in the business without needing founder intuition.

 - Work with Aviara on budget, hiring, pricing changes, tuck-in opportunities, and the first 100-day operating plan.


FIRST 90 DAYS

 - Build trust with the team, founder/seller, customers, and key local relationships.

 - Learn the business by sitting in the work, not just reviewing reports.

 - Produce a simple operating diagnostic: revenue drivers, customer segments, team strengths/gaps, margin leaks, process bottlenecks, cash issues, and AI/process opportunities.

 - Establish a weekly business review and a short list of measurable priorities.

 - Fix a few obvious problems quickly without breaking the culture.


WHAT GOOD LOOKS LIKE

 - The team knows what matters each week.

 - Sales follow-up is no longer informal or founder-dependent.

 - Customer issues are visible and acted on.

 - Cash, receivables, pricing, and margins are watched closely.

 - Manual workflows are getting turned into repeatable systems.

 - Aviara can understand business performance from clean weekly updates.

 - The business is more organized, more commercial, and more scalable six months after you start.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 3-8 years of experience in operations, sales leadership, business operations, startups, consulting, private equity portfolio operati

source_jobs.title: Fan Page Content Creator for Music Artist
source_jobs.description:
We’re looking for talented editors and creatives who can run series-led “fan pages” (or owned pages) for an independent pop artist.

These can be creator-driven, editing-driven, or hook-driven.

The primary POV we are exploring is the intern POV — a snarky intern who promotes the artist because the artist refuses to post herself. The intern’s voice is chronically online, self-aware, and severely unhinged.



What You’ll Do

 - Post several times daily across fan pages

 - Build engagement through captions, comments, and trends

 - Maintain the fan page’s aesthetic and voice

 - Track growth and pitch fresh content ideas



What We’re Looking For

 - Strong knowledge of TikTok/Instagram/YouTube trends

 - Experience editing short-form content (CapCut, Reels, Shorts, etc.)

 - Creative, fast, and organized

 - Passion for pop music and fan culture



Perks

 - Work directly with the artist’s team

 - Flexible, remote, part-time role

 - Opportunity to help shape a global fan community




EXAMPLE CONCEPTS

Creator / Organic-Driven Content

This type of content involves filming oneself so that the POV feels realistic (showing one’s face and/or voice is optional).

 - Day X of copying my boss’s music videos until she notices

 - Day X of promoting my boss’s music in public bathrooms until I get caught

 - Day X of writing letters to inmates using only my boss’s lyrics

 - Day X of drawing my favorite artist until she hires me for her album artwork



Editing-Driven Content

This type of content uses editing of existing content to create a standard template for a recurring series.

 - Day X of replacing my boss’s face with X until she notices
   
   (Note: X can be a famous person, DJ, or influencer — ideally someone chronically online. This would most likely use a horizontal music video clip as the asset.)

 - Day X of translating my boss’s lyrics into “Chinese”
   
   (Note: The translation is intentionally wrong — that’s the joke. This would most likely use a horizontal music video clip as the asset. Any language could be used.)

 - Day X of shipping my boss with X (so I can get backstage passes)
   
   (Note: X should be a famous artist or celebrity. This is framed as shipping/parody, not real gossip.)



Hook-Driven Content

This type of content involves adding a simple but scroll-worthy hook on top of existing content.

Horizontal music video clip with copy above, for example:

 - Day X of trying to get X to remix my boss’s song (so I can meet booth baddies)
   
   (Note: X should be a famous DJ — ideally someone chronically online.)

 - Day X of trying to convince my sister’s favorite artist to perform at her wedding

BTS clip with copy on top, for example:

 - Watch my boss X
   
   (Note: X should be something where the intern is roasting the boss.)

source_jobs.title: Lifecycle Manager
source_jobs.description:
We are looking for a data-driven, creative, and highly technical Lifecycle Manager to own our customer retention strategy. Your primary playground will be Klaviyo, and your North Star metric will be Customer Lifetime Value (LTV).

This isn't a role for a generalist—we need someone who has lived and breathed e-commerce, understands the nuances of a high-growth brand, and knows how to turn a one-time buyer into a lifelong advocate.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Klaviyo Mastery: Own the full execution of our email and SMS strategy. This includes sophisticated segmentation, high-converting automated flows (Welcome, Abandoned Cart, Post-Purchase, Win-back), and a high-frequency campaign calendar.

 - LTV Optimization: Analyze customer cohorts to identify churn points and opportunities for upsells or cross-sells. You will be responsible for moving the needle on repeat purchase rates and average order value (AOV).

 - A/B Testing & Optimization: Maintain a rigorous testing roadmap. You’ll constantly experiment with subject lines, send times, content blocks, and incentive structures to squeeze every ounce of ROI out of our list.

 - Personalization & Segmentation: Move beyond "batch and blast." Use predictive analytics and behavioral data to deliver the right message to the right person at the right time.

 - Collaboration: Work closely with the Creative team to ensure every touchpoint is on-brand and high-converting, and with the Product team to align lifecycle triggers with the on-site experience.

 - Reporting: Deliver weekly and monthly reports on channel performance, attribution, and health of the subscriber base.


WHO YOU ARE

 - E-comm Native: You have 3+ years of experience working in-house for a direct-to-consumer (DTC) or e-commerce brand. You understand the unique challenges of inventory cycles, seasonal peaks, and customer psychology.

 - Klaviyo Expert: You don't just "know" Klaviyo; you’re a power user. You understand custom properties, event-based triggers, and how to integrate Klaviyo with the rest of our tech stack (Shopify, Gorgias, etc.).

 - Analytical Mindset: You are comfortable in a spreadsheet. You can calculate LTV, Paybakc period, etc in your sleep and use data to tell a story.

 - Copy & Creative Eye: While you don't need to be a designer, you know what "good" looks like. You can write punchy, brand-aligned copy that drives action.

 - Self-Starter: You are comfortable in a fast-paced environment where you own your results. You don't wait for a brief; you find the opportunity and build the solution.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Proven track record of increasing Retention/LTV at a growth-stage e-commerce brand.

 - Deep technical proficiency in Klaviyo (Certification is a plus).

 - Experience with Shopify

 - Familiarity with SMS marketing platforms (Attentive, Postscript, or Klaviyo SMS).

 - Strong understanding of deliverability, list hygiene, and GDPR/CCPA compliance.

source_jobs.title: Deal Origination Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT AVIARA

Aviara is a private equity shop focused on AI-enabled rollups in emerging markets. We buy durable, founder-led businesses where local trust already exists, then improve them with better operating cadence, sales discipline, AI automation, and sharper financial management.

Our initial focus is Argentina and Latin America. We are looking at service businesses, software-enabled services, professional services, niche B2B operators, and legacy local companies where the owner has built something real but the business still runs on manual workflows, informal sales, spreadsheets, WhatsApp, and founder memory.

We are not looking for passive analysts. We need people who can find owners, get meetings, keep a pipeline moving, and turn messy market information into actionable acquisition opportunities.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Deal Origination Associate, your job is to create deal flow.

You will build company lists, find owner contact information, write outreach, call founders, follow up relentlessly, qualify businesses, and keep clean notes in the pipeline. You will spend a lot of time doing direct outreach. You should be comfortable with rejection, ambiguity, and unstructured markets.

This is a tactical hunting role. A great week means you found new targets, contacted owners, got replies, booked calls, learned something useful about a market, and moved qualified opportunities forward. The role can grow into diligence or operating work, but the first job is simple: find businesses worth buying and get us in conversation with the owners.


WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Build target lists from scratch using Google, LinkedIn, local directories, industry associations, broker listings, WhatsApp groups, referrals, registries, and any other source that works.

 - Find real owner, founder, CEO, broker, and decision-maker contact information.

 - Send cold email, LinkedIn, WhatsApp, and phone outreach in Spanish and English.

 - Make follow-up part of the job, not an afterthought. Track every touch, next step, and response.

 - Book introductory calls with owners, brokers, and operators.

 - Ask practical first-call questions: revenue range, customer type, owner involvement, reason for considering a sale, team size, margin profile, growth constraints, software/tools used, and what the business depends on.

 - Keep the CRM clean: company profile, owner contact, source, status, next action, notes, financial snippets, strategic fit, and risks.

 - Build weekly market maps for specific acquisition themes, such as legal services, accounting firms, field services, specialty B2B services, software-enabled agencies, local vertical SaaS, logistics, compliance, healthcare admin, education services, or other fragmented categories.

 - Write short target memos that explain what the company does, why the owner might sell, how the business makes money, what could break, and where AI or operating improvements could matter.

 - Support early diligence by collecting financials, customer information, org charts, product/service details, and operating workflows.

 - Help us identify repeatable acquisition theses, not just random one-off companies.


WHAT GOOD LOOKS LIKE

 - You are adding new qualified companies to the pipeline every week.

 - You are personally creating conversations with owners, not waiting for inbound leads.

 - Your notes are crisp enough that someone else can pick up a conversation without asking you what happened.

 - You know which verticals are producing responses and which are wasting time.

 - You can explain why a business is interesting in plain language, without hiding behind generic finance words.

 - You are persistent, direct, and professional with owners.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 1-3 years of experience in sales, business development, search funds, private equity, investment banking, consulting, corporate development, startups, recruiting, or any role where you had to source opportunities and get people to respond.

 - Strong Spanis

我们是一家在深圳和纽约运营的初创公司，正在打造 AI 智能体和消费级硬件设备。用户可以通过智能体在不同工具中执行操作，我们也在开发能够随时捕捉用户意图的定制设备。团队规模精简，创始人曾创办一家硅谷独角兽公司。

我们正在寻找一位硬件采购经理，负责为多个客户项目寻找、评估并协调大湾区的硬件工厂、设备供应商和制造合作伙伴。你将经常前往深圳、东莞、广州、珠海、佛山及周边产业带，把客户需求推进为可执行的打样、生产和交付计划。


工作内容

 - 走访硬件工厂、组装厂、模具厂、电子元器件供应商和设备供应商。

 - 根据不同客户的产品需求、预算、质量标准和时间表筛选供应商。

 - 收集报价、规格、认证、产能、起订量、交期和付款条件，整理中英文对比和建议。

 - 协调 NDA、RFQ、打样、工程验证、工厂审核、试产、量产、质量检查、包装和物流。

 - 现场检查样品、生产线和关键工艺，拍摄照片和视频，并及时汇报。

 - 同时推进多个客户项目，跟踪风险、决策、截止时间和下一步行动。

 - 与客户、工程师、工业设计师、工厂负责人和物流合作伙伴保持清晰沟通。


任职要求

 - 常驻深圳或大湾区，能够频繁前往深圳、东莞、广州、珠海、佛山及周边工厂。

 - 有消费电子、智能硬件、IoT、可穿戴设备、电子元器件、模具或组装采购经验。

 - 能够独立完成供应商搜寻、询价、谈判、工厂走访、样品跟进和项目协调。

 - 理解打样、BOM、DFM、EVT、DVT、PVT 等硬件开发和制造流程。

 - 中文沟通能力强，并能使用英文进行客户更新、书面总结和会议沟通。

 - 做事有条理、反应快，能够同时管理多个客户项目。


加分项

 - 在大湾区拥有成熟的硬件工厂、电子市场、模具、SMT、组装或设备供应商网络。

 - 有从概念、样品到量产和交付的完整项目经验。

 - 熟悉质量体系、工厂审核、产品认证、进出口或跨境物流。


申请说明

请说明你做过的硬件或设备采购项目、你熟悉的工厂和产业带、你能够覆盖的大湾区城市，以及你如何同时协调多个客户项目。如有相关项目案例，请附上链接。


ROLE OVERVIEW

We are a startup building AI agents and consumer devices. Users prompt agents to take actions across their tools, while our custom devices capture intent on the go. We are a small team in Shenzhen and New York, and our founder previously started a Silicon Valley unicorn.

We are looking for a Hardware Sourcing Manager to identify, evaluate, and coordinate hardware factories, device suppliers, and manufacturing partners for projects across multiple clients. You will travel frequently throughout the Greater Bay Area, including Shenzhen, Dongguan, Guangzhou, Zhuhai, Foshan, and nearby manufacturing hubs.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Visit hardware factories, assembly plants, tooling shops, component suppliers, and equipment vendors.

 - Source suppliers against each client's product requirements, budget, quality bar, and timeline.

 - Collect quotes, specifications, certifications, capacity, MOQs, lead times, and payment terms, then produce clear comparisons in English and Chinese.

 - Coordinate NDAs, RFQs, prototypes, engineering validation, factory audits, pilot runs, mass production, quality inspections, packaging, and logistics.

 - Inspect samples and production lines in person and document findings with organized photos, videos, and written updates.

 - Run several client projects in parallel and track owners, risks, decisions, deadlines, and next actions.

 - Communicate clearly with clients, engineers, industrial designers, factory managers, and logistics partners.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in Shenzhen or the Greater Bay Area and able to travel frequently across Shenzhen, Dongguan, Guangzhou, Zhuhai, Foshan, and nearby cities.

 - Hands-on sourcing experience in consumer electronics, smart hardware, IoT, wearables, components, tooling, or assembly.

 - Able to independently source vendors, run RFQs, negotiate terms, conduct factory visits, and coordinate projects.

 - Working knowledge of prototyping, BOMs, DFM, EVT, DVT, and PVT.

 - Strong Chinese and working English for client updates, written summaries, and meetings.

 - Organized, responsive, and comfortable managing multiple clients and changing priorities.


HELPFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED

 - An existing network of hardware factories, electronics markets, tooling, SMT, assembly, or equipment suppliers in the Greater Bay Area.

 - Experience taking a product from concept and prototype through mass production and delivery.

 - Familiarity with quality systems, factory audits, product certifications, import and export, or cross-border logistics.


HOW TO APPLY

Please describe hardware or device sourcing projects you have handled, the factories and manufacturing categories you know, the Greater Bay Area cities you can cover, and how you coordinate several client projects at once. Include relevant project links where available.

source_jobs.description:
职位概况

我们正在寻找一位位于佛山或周边地区的兼职合同制家具采购与物流代理，协助一个定制住宅项目完成家具、家居材料及相关物流的实地采购工作。你将根据设计师和业主家庭的需求，走访佛山及周边家具市场、展厅、工厂、仓库和供应商，收集报价、照片、视频和规格信息，并协助推进下单、付款、验货、包装、运输和交付安排。


工作内容

 - 走访佛山及周边城市的家具市场、展厅、工厂、仓库和供应商。

 - 根据设计需求、预算、尺寸、材质和时间要求寻找合适家具选项。

 - 拍摄清晰照片和视频，收集报价，确认规格，并整理选项供业主审核。

 - 在合适情况下协助谈价和确认条款，帮助筛选可靠供应商。

 - 协调下单、付款、验货、包装、本地运输、出口或国内物流以及交付时间。

 - 用英文和中文与业主家庭、设计师、供应商和物流方清晰沟通。

 - 用简单共享表格或文档跟踪价格、联系人、决策和下一步事项。


任职要求

 - 常驻佛山，或能够经常前往佛山家具市场和供应商处。

 - 中文沟通能力强；具备用于更新、总结和电话沟通的英文能力。

 - 有家具采购、室内设计采购、出口物流、买手或采购代理等实地采购经验。

 - 对质量、材质、饰面、尺寸和实际交付细节有判断力。

 - 能够独立走访供应商，快速推进，并提供有条理的更新。

 - 做事可靠透明，能谨慎处理报价、付款、时间线和供应商沟通。


加分项

 - 在佛山、乐从、顺德或周边家具产业带有现成供应商资源。

 - 有定制家具、住宅装修项目或国际运输经验。

 - 能够检查样品、饰面、软包、木材、石材、金属、灯具或相关家居材料。


申请问题

 - 请说明你通常能前往佛山家具市场或供应商处的频率。

 - 如有相关项目照片、供应商案例、采购表格或作品集，请附上链接。


ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a part-time contract furniture sourcing and logistics agent based in Foshan or nearby. This is a one-time project for a custom family home after the design is complete. You will help source furniture and related home materials, visit suppliers in person, collect quotes and specifications, and coordinate the practical details from selection through ordering, inspection, packing, transport, and delivery.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Visit furniture markets, showrooms, factories, warehouses, and suppliers in Foshan and nearby cities.

 - Source furniture options that match the design brief, budget, dimensions, materials, and timing.

 - Take clear photos and videos, collect quotes, confirm specifications, and organize options for review.

 - Negotiate pricing and terms when appropriate, and help identify reliable suppliers.

 - Coordinate ordering, payments, inspections, packing, local transport, export or domestic logistics, and delivery timing.

 - Communicate clearly in English and Chinese with the family, designers, vendors, and logistics providers.

 - Track details in a simple shared spreadsheet or document so decisions, prices, contacts, and next steps stay organized.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in Foshan or able to travel frequently to Foshan furniture markets and suppliers.

 - Strong spoken and written Chinese; working English for updates, summaries, and calls.

 - Experience in furniture sourcing, interior design procurement, export logistics, buying agency work, or similar hands-on sourcing work.

 - Good eye for quality, materials, finishes, dimensions, and practical delivery details.

 - Comfortable moving quickly, visiting suppliers independently, and sending organized updates.

 - Reliable, transparent, and careful with pricing, payments, timelines, and vendor communication.


HELPFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED

 - Existing supplier contacts in Foshan, Lecong, Shunde, or nearby furniture hubs.

 - Experience with custom furniture, home renovation projects, or international shipping.

 - Ability to inspect samples, finishes, upholstery, wood, stone, metalwork, lighting, or related home materials.


APPLICATION QUESTIONS

 - Please apply with a short note about your relevant sourcing, furniture, supplier coordination, or logistics experience.

 - Please mention how often you can visit Foshan furniture markets or suppliers.

 - If available, include links to relevant project photos, supplier examples, procurement sheets, or portfolio materials.

 - 走访佛山及周边城市的家具市场、展厅、工厂、仓库和供应商。

 - 根据设计需求、预算、尺寸、材质和时间要求寻找合适家具选项。

 - 拍摄清晰照片和视频，收集报价，确认规格，并整理选项供业主审核。

 - 在合适情况下协助谈价和确认条款，帮助筛选可靠供应商。

 - 协调下单、付款、验货、包装、本地运输、出口或国内物流以及交付时间。

 - 用英文和中文与业主家庭、设计师、供应商和物流方清晰沟通。

 - 用简单共享表格或文档跟踪价格、联系人、决策和下一步事项。


任职要求

 - 常驻佛山，或能够经常前往佛山家具市场和供应商处。

 - 中文沟通能力强；具备用于更新、总结和电话沟通的英文能力。

 - 有家具采购、室内设计采购、出口物流、买手或采购代理等实地采购经验。

 - 对质量、材质、饰面、尺寸和实际交付细节有判断力。

 - 能够独立走访供应商，快速推进，并提供有条理的更新。

 - 做事可靠透明，能谨慎处理报价、付款、时间线和供应商沟通。


加分项

 - 在佛山、乐从、顺德或周边家具产业带有现成供应商资源。

 - 有定制家具、住宅装修项目或国际运输经验。

 - 能够检查样品、饰面、软包、木材、石材、金属、灯具或相关家居材料。


申请问题

 - 请简要说明你与家具采购、供应商协调或物流相关的经验。

 - 请说明你通常能前往佛山家具市场或供应商处的频率。

 - 如有相关项目照片、供应商案例、采购表格或作品集，请附上链接。


ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a part-time contract furniture sourcing and logistics agent based in Foshan or nearby. This is a one-time project for a custom family home after the design is complete. You will help source furniture and related home materials, visit suppliers in person, collect quotes and specifications, and coordinate the practical details from selection through ordering, inspection, packing, transport, and delivery.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Visit furniture markets, showrooms, factories, warehouses, and suppliers in Foshan and nearby cities.

 - Source furniture options that match the design brief, budget, dimensions, materials, and timing.

 - Take clear photos and videos, collect quotes, confirm specifications, and organize options for review.

 - Negotiate pricing and terms when appropriate, and help identify reliable suppliers.

 - Coordinate ordering, payments, inspections, packing, local transport, export or domestic logistics, and delivery timing.

 - Communicate clearly in English and Chinese with the family, designers, vendors, and logistics providers.

 - Track details in a simple shared spreadsheet or document so decisions, prices, contacts, and next steps stay organized.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in Foshan or able to travel frequently to Foshan furniture markets and suppliers.

 - Strong spoken and written Chinese; working English for updates, summaries, and calls.

 - Experience in furniture sourcing, interior design procurement, export logistics, buying agency work, or similar hands-on sourcing work.

 - Good eye for quality, materials, finishes, dimensions, and practical delivery details.

 - Comfortable moving quickly, visiting suppliers independently, and sending organized updates.

 - Reliable, transparent, and careful with pricing, payments, timelines, and vendor communication.


HELPFUL BUT NOT REQUIRED

 - Existing supplier contacts in Foshan, Lecong, Shunde, or nearby furniture hubs.

 - Experience with custom furniture, home renovation projects, or international shipping.

 - Ability to inspect samples, finishes, upholstery, wood, stone, metalwork, lighting, or related home materials.


APPLICATION QUESTIONS

 - Please apply with a short note about your relevant sourcing, furniture, supplier coordination, or logistics experience.

 - Please mention how often you can visit Foshan furniture markets or suppliers.

 - If available, include links to relevant project photos, supplier examples, procurement sheets, or portfolio materials.

source_jobs.title: Brand Partnerships Manager 品牌合作经理
source_jobs.description:
Italic 正在深圳招聘一名商务拓展与合作经理，负责接触和筛选中国品牌，推动他们通过 Italic 进入美国市场。这个岗位会和中国品牌方、工厂、创始人及渠道团队沟通，判断品牌和产品是否适合美国消费者，并推动从初次接触、商业方案、样品评估到合作落地的全过程。

我们希望候选人有 3-5 年零售、电商、品牌方、平台或分销相关的 BD、商务拓展、销售或合作伙伴经验。这个角色适合喜欢主动开拓、能把复杂合作讲清楚、并且能在中美商业语境之间切换的人。


工作职责

 - 主动寻找、接触和跟进适合进入美国市场的中国消费品牌，重点关注家居、生活方式、服饰配件、美妆个护、消费电子及其他高潜力品类。

 - 向品牌方清楚介绍 Italic 的美国市场分销、渠道和品牌增长机会，判断对方的需求、能力和合作意向。

 - 建立并维护品牌候选名单、联系人、跟进状态、样品进度、商业条款和下一步行动。

 - 和品牌方讨论基础合作条款，包括产品范围、供货能力、价格区间、渠道限制、市场支持和美国分销安排。

 - 协调内部团队评估品牌和产品机会，包括产品质量、价格竞争力、供应稳定性、包装、合规风险和美国市场适配度。

 - 推动从冷启动外联、初次会议、样品寄送、资料收集、商业沟通到合作落地的完整流程。

 - 整理市场和品牌情报，持续发现中国品牌出海、跨境电商、DTC、Amazon、TikTok Shop 和零售渠道中的新机会。

 - 和深圳及美国团队保持清晰沟通，及时同步进展、风险、阻塞点和需要决策的事项。


任职要求

 - 常驻深圳，能够线下拜访品牌方、供应商、展会或合作伙伴。

 - 3-5 年 BD、商务拓展、销售、渠道合作、品牌合作或平台招商经验，优先考虑零售、电商、消费品牌、跨境电商或分销相关背景。

 - 有主动开发客户或合作伙伴的经验，能独立完成外联、会议沟通、跟进和推进。

 - 理解中国品牌出海到美国市场时常见的问题，包括定位、渠道、定价、包装、合规、库存和品牌信任。

 - 沟通清楚、反应快，能够把 Italic 的合作模式讲得具体、有说服力。

 - 有较强的商业判断力，能快速判断一个品牌是否值得继续推进。

 - 做事有条理，能长期维护大量线索、联系人和合作进度。

 - 普通话流利，能用英文进行书面沟通；如果能进行英文商务会议更好。


加分项

 - 有跨境电商、Amazon、Shopify、TikTok Shop、海外分销或美国零售渠道经验。

 - 有消费品品牌方、贸易公司、平台招商、渠道销售或品牌代理经验。

 - 熟悉深圳、广州、东莞、义乌、杭州等地的品牌、供应链或展会资源。

 - 做过品牌出海、美国市场进入、分销代理、渠道合作或商务谈判。

 - 能用 CRM、表格或其他工具清晰管理线索和销售漏斗。

Italic is hiring a Business Development & Partnerships Manager in Shenzhen to identify and work with Chinese brands that want to enter the U.S. market, with Italic acting as their U.S. distributor. This role owns outreach, qualification, relationship management, commercial discussion, sample coordination, and the handoff from promising brand lead to active partnership.

We are looking for someone with 3-5 years of BD, partnerships, sales, channel, retail, e-commerce, brand, or distribution experience. The right person is proactive, commercially sharp, organized, and comfortable moving between Chinese brand teams and a U.S.-market strategy.


RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Source, contact, and follow up with Chinese consumer brands that may be a fit for U.S. market entry through Italic.

 - Explain Italic's U.S. distribution and market-entry opportunity clearly to founders, brand teams, factories, and channel partners.

 - Build and maintain a pipeline of target brands, contacts, follow-up status, samples, commercial terms, and next steps.

 - Discuss early partnership terms, including product scope, supply capacity, pricing, channel restrictions, market support, and U.S. distribution needs.

 - Coordinate internal review of brand and product opportunities across product quality, pricing, supply reliability, packaging, compliance risk, and U.S. market fit.

 - Own the full early-stage BD workflow from outbound outreach and first calls to sample collection, materials review, commercial discussion, and partnership handoff.

 - Track market and brand intelligence across Chinese consumer brands, cross-border e-commerce, DTC, Amazon, TikTok Shop, and retail channels.

 - Keep the Shenzhen and U.S. teams aligned with clear updates on progress, risks, blockers, and decisions needed.


REQUIREMENTS

 - Based in Shenzhen and able to meet brands, suppliers, trade-show contacts, and partners in person.

 - 3-5 years of experience in BD, partnerships, sales, channel partnerships, brand partnerships, marketplace development, retail, e-commerce, consumer brands, cross-border commerce, or distribution.

 - Experience proactively sourcing and developing customers or partners from initial outreach through follow-up and next-step execution.

 - Good understanding of the challenges Chinese brands face when entering the U.S. market, including positioning, channel strategy, pricing, packaging, compliance, inventory, and trust.

 - Clear communicator who can make Italic's partnership model feel concrete and credible.

 - Strong commercial judgment and the ability to quickly assess whether a brand is worth pursuing.

 - Organized enough to manage a large volume of leads, contacts, and partnership conversations over time.

 - Fluent Mandarin and strong written English; spoken business English is a plus.

notes: Multiple job postings for various roles, including tech and non-tech positions. Company appears to be a startup with a mix of engineering, marketing, and operations roles.
